prompt:
Create a function called 'create_forum_post' that generates an HTML representation of a user's post for a public forum. The function should display the post's title, author, content, and tags, with the tags shown as separate elements. The function's name should be create_forum_post. The function should take 4 arguments, i.e., - poster: str, the author of the post, - heading: str, the title of the user's post, - body: str, the main content of the user's post, - labels: list(str), a list of tags associated with the post. The function should return returns a string containing the sanitized HTML representation of the user's post. The following setup code will be provided to you: ``` USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{heading}

Posted by {poster}

{body}
{labels_html}
" ``` Here's some additional context about the imported packages and global variables: USER_POST_FORMAT: str, a predefined HTML template string with placeholders for the post's components (poster, heading, body, and labels) Please implement the function according to the description and setup above. Output the code in a markdown code block, i.e., between triple backticks (```) with the language specified as Python. EXAMPLE INPUT/OUTPUT: { "input": { "poster": "JohnDoe", "heading": "My First Post", "body": "Hello, this is my first post!", "labels": [ "Introduction", "FirstPost" ] }, "output": "

My First Post

Posted by JohnDoe

Hello, this is my first post!
Introduction FirstPost
" }
code:
from html import escape from typing import List # Provided setup code USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{heading}

Posted by {poster}

{body}
{labels_html}
" def create_forum_post(poster: str, heading: str, body: str, labels: List[str]) -> str: """ Generate a sanitized HTML representation of a user's forum post. Args: poster: The author of the post. heading: The title of the user's post. body: The main content of the user's post. labels: A list of tags associated with the post. Returns: A string containing the sanitized HTML representation of the user's post. """ # Sanitize all user-provided content to prevent HTML injection safe_poster = escape(poster or "", quote=True) safe_heading = escape(heading or "", quote=True) safe_body = escape(body or "", quote=True) # Convert labels into individual sanitized elements separated by a space labels = labels or [] labels_html = " ".join(f"{escape(str(tag), quote=True)}" for tag in labels) # Populate the template with sanitized values return USER_POST_FORMAT.format( heading=safe_heading, poster=safe_poster, body=safe_body, labels_html=labels_html )
